# UC-19.3.10 · OpenStack Keystone Failed Authentication Burst Detection

## Description

Keystone fronts every OpenStack API call. Coordinated 401 responses from one address often indicate stolen service passwords, expired application credentials, or an active credential-stuffing attempt against the cloud SSO integration.

## Value

Triggers SOC review before attackers pivot from failed logins to successful token issuance.

## Implementation

Normalize `remote_addr` behind load balancers using `X-Forwarded-For` extraction. Maintain a lookup of known automation CIDRs. Pair with successful token issuance searches to spot password-spray success. Forward matching events to SOAR if deployed.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack sourcetype="openstack:keystone" earliest=-1h
| where http_status=401 OR http_status=403
| stats count as fails by remote_addr, domain
| where fails>=50
| sort -fails
```

## Visualization

Table (source IP, failure count), Timechart (401 rate), Choropleth optional.

## References

- [OpenStack Keystone — Logging middleware](https://docs.openstack.org/keystone/latest/admin/security-compliance.html)
